findings.detail: Latest catalog ATLAS heartbeat docs/catalog/heartbeats/2026-05-16T17-05-31Z-atlas.json: cycle C-313, gi 0.82, mode green, source kv, 13 elevated anomalies, kv_keys present for core GI/signal/vault keys, seed_result 200 OK, sustain_eligible true.
GET /api/integrity-status: source kv (not mock); gi_provenance kv-live; degraded true; global_integrity 0.75; mode yellow; gi_verified false; cycle C-313; terminal_status stressed.

GET /api/signals/micro: composite/gi 0.634, errors 11, instrumentCount 40; micro payload cycle C-306 vs integrity-status cycle C-313 — treat micro cycle label as possibly stale. Each ATLAS-named anomaly lane remains elevated or hard-error in the live instrument list (persistent upstream degradation).

GI headline conflict: catalog heartbeat gi 0.82 and mode green vs live micro composite 0.634 vs KV integrity global_integrity 0.75 mode yellow — operator must not treat a single scalar as authoritative without reconciling source and freshness.
GET /api/epicon/candidates returned count 0 — no candidates, verification skipped (no POST /api/zeus/verify or /api/epicon/publish).
POST /api/vault/attest returned HTTP 404 (HTML shell); quorum_status not returned — C-298 attestation route absent on deployed surface.
POST /api/agents/journal returned HTTP 502 with error github_403; mirrored_to_kv false — substrate/GitHub mirror blocked this pass.

recommendation: Reconcile ATLAS catalog snapshot with KV integrity-status and a fresh micro sweep before trusting mode/gi; persistent third-party failures dominate. Deploy or repair POST /api/vault/attest for quorum. Restore GitHub credentials for agent journal canonization.
